figures.caption: Figure 4: (a) Per-dimension ARR averaged across layers. Four Hybrid-Recoverable (HR) dimensions achieve ≥85% recovery, while ImgQual is Hybrid-Sensitive (HS) at ∼25%, motivating HS-aware layer selection to minimize quality degradation. (b) Each point corresponds to one of the 30 layers, plotted by ∆HR (x-axis) and ∆HS (y-axis). Here the 50% lowest-pℓ layers (blue) are selected for replacement. (c) End-to-end validation after Stage 2: the hybrid model matches or exceeds the baseline on all HR dimensions and recovers ImgQual to within ∼1%, indicating that HS-aware selection preserves sensitive dimensions while allowing HR gaps to be closed via joint distillation.
Table 5: Stress-test prompts for sensitivity-guided layer selection. Each prompt targets a specific state weakness axis: TC = temporal consistency, DY = motion dynamics, SQ = spatial quality.
Table 6: Detailed VBench per-dimension scores. ‡: native resolution 544×960, 97 frames.

Figure 1: (a) AR video diffusion relies on softmax self-attention with a growing KV cache, incurring O(N) memory, which limits scalability. (b) Replacing self-attention with our hybrid linear attention, the model maintains a fixed-size recurrent state for streaming context with O(1) memory.

Table 1: ARL2 achieves comparable overall performance to representative open-source video models, while improving temporal flickering and background consistency, with O(1) memory complexity. ARL2 (50%/75%) denotes replacing 50%/75% of attention layers with hybrid linear attention.
Table 2: ARL2 achieves consistent efficiency gains with up to 2.26× speedup and significantly reduced memory, while maintaining competitive or improved video quality.

Figure 2: ARL2 attention module (normalization omitted for clarity). Given tokens XN of frame N , QN , KN , VN , which are routed to two branches. The intra-frame branch (top) applies bidirectional softmax attention over tokens within the current frame, producing Ointra. The inter-frame branch (bottom) applies recurrent linear attention, where a fixed-size state S maintains and updates longrange memory across frames, and produces Ointer. The state S is updated once per frame. During training, the hybrid module is learned via a progressive two-stage distillation pipeline (See Sec. 3.4).

Figure 3: (a) Softmax attention scales quadratically with video length, while the hybrid layer scales linearly. (b) The KV cache grows linearly with video length, whereas ours remains constant. (c) The recurrent state S is shared across all tokens within a frame and updated only after the final clean pass.

Figure 7: Qualitative comparison across all evaluated models. Six uniformly sampled frames from 81-frame videos generated with the prompt “A 3D model of a 1800s victorian house.” ARL2 variants maintain consistent appearance and structural detail comparable to the full-softmax baselines while operating with significantly reduced memory and compute.
